title: Amazon stops accepting new Mechanical Turk customers
friendlyTitle: Amazon is putting Mechanical Turk into maintenance mode
summary: TechCrunch reports that Amazon will stop accepting new Mechanical Turk customers from July 30, while existing users can continue and AWS maintains security and availability. The service, once central to microtask labor and data annotation, will receive no new features.
friendlySummary: New customers will no longer be able to sign up for Mechanical Turk after July 30. Existing customers can still use it, but Amazon is not planning new features.
whyItMatters: Mechanical Turk helped define the hidden human labor layer behind early automation and AI datasets. Its move into maintenance mode signals a shift toward more specialized data-labeling platforms, synthetic data, and AI-assisted annotation workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because many AI systems were built with help from human microtasks. The tools for labeling data and checking AI work are changing.
